free_text: Investigation of the (d,p) reaction on magnesium
isotopes at low deuteron energies.
The 2.5 MeV Van de Graaff electrostatic
accelerator.
Two n-Si semi-conductor detectors placed inside
the target chamber were used.  One of them having an
area of 0.2 cm2 and solid angle 5.3*10**(-5) sr served
as a monitor at an angle 135 degr. with respect to the
incident beam. The other detector with the area 0.5 cm2
and solid angle 1.68*10**(-3)sr could be rotated around
the target in the range from 30 to 150 degr. with
respect to the incident deuteron beam.
SD: Small corrections in all Subents.
Author's name corrected.
A target was prepared by evaporating natural
magnesium in a vacuum, onto a thin silver backing.
The backing was prepared also by evaporating silver
in a vacuum, onto a glass plate previously dipped in
a soap solution. A transparent silver film was easily
removed on water and glued to the target holder. The
silver backing was 0.05-0.2 um thick.
Fig.4 from Nucl.Phys.,56(1964)97
SD: STATUS updated. Accuracy of digitized
data presentation revised. ARB-UNITS -> NO-DIM in DATA.

SD: STATUS updated. Accuracy of digitized

A self-supporting isotopic target of 26Mg was 0.85
mg/cm2 thick. Another isotopic target of 26Mg several
times thinner was prepared on a thin carbon backing.
The isotopic targets contained 87.3% 26Mg, 11.4% 24Mg
and 1.3% 25Mg.
Fig.9 from Nucl.Phys.,56(1964)97
